chunks.text: IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH CIRCUIT COURT, LARKANA Present: Irshad Ali Shah, J. Agha Faisal, J. CP D606of 2013: Nihal Khan TagarVs. Government of Sindh & others For the Petitioner: In person Date of hearing: 03.03.2022 Date of announcement: 03.03.2022 ORDER Agha Faisal, J. The petitioner claims to have been acontingent/ contractual employee in 2010 and seeks regularization of services. The respondents have submitted that the petitioner is not an employee thereof and has no right to seek regularization. Admittedly, there is no existing relationship between the parties. The august Supreme Court has maintained in Khushal Khan that the High Court lacked jurisdiction to revive, amend or alter contracts; there was no vested right to seek regularization for employees hired on contractual basis unless there was legal and statutory basis for the same; contractual employees had no automatic right to be regularized unless the same has specifically been provided for in a law; and that the relationship of contractual employees is governed by principles of master and servant. A Division Bench of this Court has held in Anjum Badar that contractual employees had no vested right for regular appointment or to seek regularization of their services, hence, were debarred from invoking the constitutional jurisdiction of this Court. It is settled law that contractual employees are devoid of any generic entitlement for regularization. Petitioner's counsel has been unable to identify any specific law conferring any right upon the petitioner to be considered for regularization. It is, thus, our deliberated view that the petitioner has failed to set forth a case for exercise of the discretionary writ jurisdiction of this Court.

Petitioner's counsel has been unable to identify any specific law conferring any right upon the petitioner to be considered for regularization. It is, thus, our deliberated view that the petitioner has failed to set forth a case for exercise of the discretionary writ jurisdiction of this Court. In view hereof, we are constrained to observe that the petition is misconceived, hence, is hereby dismissed. JUDGE JUDGE Per Ijaz ul Ahsan J in Khushal Khan Khattak University & Others vs. Jabran Ali Khan & Others reported as 2021 SCMR 977. Per Nadeem Akhtar J in Anjum Badar vs. Province of Sindh & Others reported as PLD 2021 Sindh 328. Per Ijaz ul Ahsan J in Govt of KPK Welfare Board vs. Raheel Ali Gohar & Others reported as 2020 SCMR 2068; Per Ijaz Ul Ahsan J. in Syed Iqbal Hussain Shah Gillani vs. PBC & Others reported as 2021 SCMR 425; Muhammad Fiaz Khan vs. Ajmer Khan & Another reported as 2010 SCMR 105.
